question: What is the difference between a roof restoration and a replacement?
answer: A roof restoration involves cleaning, repairing, resealing, and repainting your existing roof — bringing it back to near-new condition at a fraction of the cost of replacement. A roof replacement means removing your existing roof entirely and installing new materials. Restoration is typically 60-70% cheaper than replacement and is suitable when the underlying structure is sound. Smart Choice will always provide honest advice on which option is best for your situation.
